mechanism: SGLT2 inhibition shifts metabolism toward fatty acid oxidation → elevated ketone bodies. Combined with reduced insulin dosing or carbohydrate restriction (fasting) → ketoacid accumulation despite near-normal glucose. Diagnosis delayed because glucose is not markedly elevated.

contextualModifiers.effect: euglycemic DKA risk during prolonged fasting

sourceSnippet: FDA Safety Communication 2015; KDIGO CKD 2024; Ramadan fasting guidelines
